criteria: Entity IDs are packed into a single u32 using 20 bits for index and 12 bits for generation, with unpack() methods extracting each field via bit manipulation
create_entity() returns a new Entity from the free list if available (with current generation) or allocates a new slot (generation 0)
destroy_entity(entity) increments the generation at the entity's index and pushes the index onto the free list, returning true only for valid alive entities
is_alive(entity) performs O(1) validation by comparing the entity's generation against the current generation at that index
Calling destroy_entity() twice with the same handle returns false the second time (generation mismatch detection)
Creating and destroying 1 million entities with pre-reserved capacity completes in under 100ms
World struct owns EntityManager and provides the public API (create_entity, destroy_entity, is_alive, entity_count, reserve_entities)
Entity::NULL sentinel (u32::MAX) is provided for optional entity references, with is_null() validation
Generation overflow wraps safely (wrapping_add), with the understanding that 4096 recycles at the same index while holding a stale reference is pathological
Free list is implemented as Vec<usize> stack for O(1) amortized push/pop operations
